title: PM Ujjwala Yojana
meta_description: PM Ujjwala Yojana provides free LPG gas connection to BPL women. Check eligibility, required documents, and how to apply.

hero.one_liner: Free LPG gas connection with first refill and stove for women from BPL households to replace unhealthy cooking fuels

what_is_it: Pradhan Mantri Ujjwala Yojana (PMUY) was launched on 1 May 2016 to provide free LPG connections to women from Below Poverty Line (BPL) households. The scheme aims to replace unhealthy cooking fuels like wood, coal, and cow dung with clean LPG gas, reducing indoor air pollution and health risks for women and children.

Under PMUY 2.0 (launched 2021), the scheme was expanded to cover migrant workers, and documentation requirements were simplified. Beneficiaries receive a free LPG connection, first refill, and a hot plate/stove at no cost. Some states also provide subsidized refills. As of 2026, over 10 crore connections have been distributed.

eligibility.label: Condition
eligibility.value: No existing LPG connection in the household

eligibility.label: PMUY 2.0 addition
eligibility.value: Migrant workers can also apply with self-declaration

eligibility.label: Documents
eligibility.value: Aadhaar card, BPL ration card or SECC list, bank account passbook, passport photo

how_to_apply.title: Visit nearest LPG distributor
how_to_apply.description: Go to your nearest HP, Bharat Gas, or Indane distributor with your documents.

how_to_apply.title: Fill the Ujjwala application form
how_to_apply.description: Get KYC form and Ujjwala application form. Fill personal details, BPL/SECC details, and bank information.

how_to_apply.title: Submit documents and photo
how_to_apply.description: Submit Aadhaar, BPL card, bank passbook copy, and passport photo. The distributor verifies your details.

how_to_apply.title: Receive connection
how_to_apply.description: Once approved, you receive a free LPG connection, first gas cylinder refill, regulator, and a hot plate — all at zero cost.

important_alert.text: Under PMUY 2.0, migrant workers who don't have address proof can apply using a self-declaration. You don't need a ration card if you're in the SECC list. Check with your local distributor.

faqs.question: Is the gas cylinder also free?
faqs.answer: The first refill is free. Subsequent refills are at market price, though eligible households receive a subsidy of ₹200-300 per cylinder deposited in their bank account via DBT.

faqs.question: Can a man apply for Ujjwala?
faqs.answer: No. The connection must be in the name of an adult woman of the household. This is a key condition of the scheme.

faqs.question: What if I already have an LPG connection?
faqs.answer: If any member of your household already has an LPG connection, you cannot apply. The scheme is for households with no existing LPG connection.

faqs.question: I don't have a BPL card. Can I still apply?
faqs.answer: Under PMUY 2.0, you can apply if your name is in the SECC 2011 list, or if you belong to SC/ST, a forest dweller, or a tea garden worker. Migrant workers can apply with self-declaration.

ministry: Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas
